chapters.owner_value: The first useful proof is not a polished page. It is a workbench where the goal, current state, checks, and next action can be inspected instead of guessed.
chapters.sales_translation: For an SMB owner, this means the project starts by mapping how work really moves before anyone promises automation.
chapters.title: Foundation: make the work visible

chapters.owner_value: The system moved from work being described after the fact to work being closed with receipts, tests, blockers, and replayable checks.
chapters.sales_translation: That is the difference between an AI demo and an operating process: the claim does not move forward until the proof can follow it.
chapters.title: Proof loop: turn activity into evidence

chapters.owner_value: The useful pivots came from red evidence: private residue, unsupported lanes, stale copy, brittle workflows, and claims that needed to be blocked until proven.
chapters.sales_translation: This is where trust is earned. The process treats blocked evidence as useful signal, not as something to hide in a footnote.
chapters.title: Friction: expose the parts that were not ready

chapters.owner_value: Once a pattern worked, it moved into runbooks, scripts, tests, docs, shared language, or reusable operating rules instead of staying trapped in one session.
chapters.sales_translation: Every project should make the next project safer and faster. That is the compounding part of the Flywheel.
chapters.title: Reuse: keep lessons that survived contact with reality

chapters.owner_value: The current arc is making the proof understandable to a non-technical owner: what changed, why it was safe, where it stopped, and which lesson now carries forward.
chapters.sales_translation: Show the proof, not the dream: one bounded workflow slice, one visible control path, one lesson that compounds into the next build.
chapters.title: Story: translate the machinery into a buying journey

first_commit.subject: feat(scripts): add source_repo backfill script for all local beads DBs

latest_commit.subject: docs(evidence): align trajectory artifacts with evidence contracts [work item]

message_pack.audience: SMB owner or buyer who wants safer AI adoption without becoming an AI tooling expert.
message_pack.core_offer: Map one workflow, improve one bounded slice, prove what changed, and carry the lesson into the next build.
must_not_say: we have many commits, so trust us
all systems are supported without receipts
nextjs_storytelling_targets: App Router rooms for owner, method, developer, operator, and contact journeys
Server Components for proof manifests that should not ship private raw state to the browser
Suspense-backed proof drawers so the owner story renders before reviewer evidence
shared CSS/design tokens for proof states, yuzu method rails, and workflow maps
Playwright screenshot gates for mobile and desktop story quality
message_pack.owner_promise: The operator helps SMB owners buy their time back by finding the manual work between systems, proving one safe slice, and keeping the lesson for the next project.
page_headline_options: Buy back the time hiding between your tools.
Turn one messy workflow into one proven slice.
Make AI useful before you make it big.

proof_translation.owner_meaning: The story comes from the work that actually happened, not a fresh marketing claim.
The process exposes what is not ready before it can affect the business.
Claims move forward only when the system can show what changed.
Lessons from one repo become reusable operating material for the next repo.
proof_translation.technical_signal: runbooks, scripts, packages, and shared design grammar

story_arc.owner_question: Is this about the mess I am actually dealing with?
story_arc.visible_wording: Your business already has the data. The work is just hidden between tools.
story_arc.visual_cue: A workflow map with disconnected systems and one highlighted manual route.

story_arc.owner_question: How do we keep this from turning into a giant AI project?
story_arc.visible_wording: A slice is one bounded workflow improvement: useful enough to feel, small enough to inspect, and clear enough to stop if the proof is not there.
story_arc.visual_cue: The selected slice is pulled out of the map and placed on a workbench.

story_arc.visible_wording: If a claim is not proven, it stays blocked.
story_arc.visual_cue: A proof rail with proven, blocked, skipped-with-reason, and private states.

story_arc.owner_question: Is this real method or just a nice page?
story_arc.visible_wording: The repo history shows the pivots, blockers, and lessons that survived contact with reality.
story_arc.visual_cue: A trajectory rail that connects foundation, friction, proof loop, reuse, and current arc.

story_arc.owner_question: What is the safe first step?
story_arc.visible_wording: Map one workflow before sending secrets, customer data, or system access.
story_arc.visual_cue: A safe intake room with redacted examples and a direct operator route.

trust_objections.proof_behavior: The first slice must have a named boundary and stop condition.

trust_objections.objection: I will not know what changed.
trust_objections.proof_behavior: Each claim links to evidence or stays blocked.
trust_objections.visible_answer: Every slice has a proof rail.

trust_objections.proof_behavior: Public proof is redacted, consented, generated, or replaced.
Unsupported claims show as blocked instead of becoming copy.

trust_objections.objection: This will replace people before it understands the work.
trust_objections.proof_behavior: Human approval remains part of the workflow slice.
trust_objections.visible_answer: The first slice is small on purpose.

trust_objections.objection: My tools already do not talk to each other.
trust_objections.proof_behavior: Integration starts with one workflow path, not the whole company.
trust_objections.visible_answer: The operating map starts with the disconnected tools the owner already uses.

trust_objections.proof_behavior: Runbooks, tests, receipts, and blockers sit behind the story.
trust_objections.visible_answer: The method is visible enough to inspect.

trust_objections.proof_behavior: Tool claims require current receipts before promotion.
trust_objections.visible_answer: Fast tools go through a controlled loop.

trust_objections.objection: If it fails, I will be stuck.
trust_objections.proof_behavior: Failed proof does not become a public claim.
trust_objections.visible_answer: Stop conditions are named up front.

trust_objections.objection: I do not want to become an AI expert.
trust_objections.proof_behavior: Technical proof is available but not required to understand the offer.
trust_objections.visible_answer: The owner approves the slice; the operator manages the machinery.

visual_primitives.job: Open inside the owner workflow instead of a generic SaaS hero.
visual_primitives.name: OperatingRoomHero

visual_primitives.job: Show systems, manual handoffs, and the selected slice boundary.
visual_primitives.name: WorkflowMap

visual_primitives.job: Show before state, bounded slice, proof state, and stop condition together.
visual_primitives.name: SliceWorkbench

visual_primitives.job: Keep evidence visible without making the SMB owner decode raw receipts.
visual_primitives.name: ProofRail

visual_primitives.job: Map owner objections to visible controls and evidence behavior.
visual_primitives.name: TrustWorryMatrix

visual_primitives.job: Repeat Peel, Press, Pour as map, prove, and reuse.
visual_primitives.name: YuzuMethodRail

visual_primitives.job: Translate git history into origin, friction, proof loop, reuse, and current arc.
visual_primitives.name: TrajectoryRail

visual_primitives.job: Let reviewers inspect generated artifacts after the owner story lands.
visual_primitives.name: ProofDrawer

voice_rules: name the owner worry before naming the machinery
show proof without forcing the owner to read raw receipts
prefer human-led control language over autonomous-agent hype
make blockers visible because hidden risk is the trust killer
treat cash flow, time, follow-up, and customer experience as stronger language than AI novelty

owner_story.headline: Flywheel has a history, not just a homepage.
owner_story.plain_language: The work started with a problem, hit constraints, changed shape, and kept the parts that proved useful. That trajectory is the trust signal.
owner_story.proof_posture: The evidence is the path through the repo, not a detached claim written after the fact.

positioning_principles: show proof, do not sell the dream
translate technical movement into owner consequences
name pivots and blockers instead of smoothing them away
keep evidence available without making the SMB owner decode it
turn lessons into reusable method, copy, design, tests, or runbooks
public_claim: The public story is extracted from repository history, then translated into owner-readable trajectory without treating commit volume as the sales pitch.
